title: 10 best herbal teas for lost voice

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Purple coneflower contains antiviral compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which can help combat viral infections that may cause hoarseness or loss of voice.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, soothing a sore or irritated throat and promoting healthy vocal cord function.

Mucilage content: Purple coneflower tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with lost voice.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with lost voice because it contains immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the immune system. This helps to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that may be causing the loss of voice. The antiviral properties of Echinacea purpurea also aid in combating viral infections that can lead to a sore throat and loss of voice. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to soothe and protect the vocal cords, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside, found in Echinacea purpurea, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, allowing for a more comfortable and clear voice.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, including arabinogalactan, have immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in reducing the severity of respiratory infections and inflammation that often cause a lost voice.

Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid, a phenolic compound found in Echinacea purpurea, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, promoting a healthier vocal cord environment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are commonly used in teas to help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, promoting a healthy voice.
Roots: Echinacea purpurea roots are often used in teas due to their high concentration of compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate throat discomfort.
Leaves: Echinacea purpurea leaves are sometimes used in teas to add a subtle flavor and contribute to the herb's overall medicinal properties that support throat health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the late morning or early afternoon when the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried echinacea purpurea mixture in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature before serving and consuming within 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the mucous membranes of the throat, allowing for a faster recovery from vocal cord inflammation or strain.

Saponins: These compounds have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be causing hoarseness or lost voice, such as respiratory tract infections like bronchitis or pneumonia.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the vocal cords from further damage and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with lost voice because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the vocal cords and surrounding tissues. The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which reduces swelling and inflammation, allowing the vocal cords to heal and recover from strain or irritation. This natural remedy promotes healthy vocal function, enabling a smooth and clear voice to return. Its restorative properties make licorice teas an effective solution for those experiencing a lost or hoarse voice.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help with lost voice by providing anti-inflammatory properties that reduce throat irritation and swelling, allowing for smoother vocal cord movement.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin aids in soothing and calming the throat, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vocal cord function, which can help alleviate lost voice.
Liquiritin: Liquiritin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce throat inflammation and irritation, allowing for more efficient vocal cord function and relief from lost voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and protect the vocal cords.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant also contain glycyrrhizin, which can help alleviate throat inflammation and promote a healthy voice.
Stems: Some teas may use the stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra as a mild alternative to the roots, although the effectiveness may vary.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots to ensure a strong tea flavor and medicinal properties.
Dry the roots in a warm place for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture and preserve the active ingredients.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for easier infusion.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered root in 200ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or cold depending on personal preference and the desired effect.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause loss of voice, such as laryngitis and tonsillitis, by killing off harmful bacteria and viruses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and vocal cords, which can contribute to a lost voice and make it harder to speak or sing.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, including the throat and lungs, allowing for better breathing and easier vocalization.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with lost voice because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and vocal cords. Thyme's essential oils, particularly thymol, have antibacterial and antiviral properties that combat infections and reduce inflammation, allowing the vocal cords to heal and recover. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus and clear congestion, promoting a smooth and clear voice. This makes thyme tea a popular remedy for restoring the voice after illness or strain.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: A phenolic monoterpene found in Thymus vulgaris teas, thymol helps soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, allowing for improved vocal cord function and potentially alleviating lost voice.

Carvacrol: A phenolic terpene in Thymus vulgaris teas, carvacrol exhibits antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections and reduce throat inflammation, contributing to the recovery of lost voice.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic diterpene found in Thymus vulgaris teas, rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce throat irritation and promote healing, ultimately aiding in the recovery of lost voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that help soothe the throat and restore the voice.
Buds: They contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, leading to a restored voice.
Flowers: They are used to make a tea that helps calm the throat and promote vocal cord recovery, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a trusted herbal source or your garden.
Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers per 8 ounce cup.
Steep the measured thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the thymus vulgaris tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the thymus vulgaris tea to cool to a comfortable temperature for consumption within 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint tea's antispasmodic compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help relax the muscles in the throat and larynx, reducing inflammation and tension that can cause hoarseness.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: The menthol in peppermint tea helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to a lost voice by blocking airways and irritating the vocal cords.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and redness in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for normal vocal function to resume.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with lost voice because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the vocal cords. The menthol in peppermint tea can help calm and relax the throat muscles, reducing inflammation and swelling. This allows the vocal cords to heal and recover from strain or irritation, restoring a normal voice. Additionally, peppermint's natural expectorant properties can help clear mucus and phlegm from the throat, further aiding in the recovery of a lost voice.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps soothe and calm the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and irritation that may be causing the lost voice.
Menthone: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and other debris that may be obstructing the vocal cords and contributing to the lost voice.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce swelling and damage to the vocal cords, promoting healing and recovery of the lost voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part for making teas to address a lost voice is the leaves, as they contain menthol and menthone, which help to ease respiratory issues and soothe the vocal cords.
Stems: Some herbal teas use the stems of Mentha x piperita to make a soothing brew, as they contain astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation in the throat and vocal cords.

Leaves: Although leaves are the primary choice, some people also use the leaves for making teas to help recover from lost voice, as they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote vocal cord health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves when they are in their best flavor and aroma by cutting them with scissors.
Rinse the peppermint leaves with cool water to remove dirt and debris from the leaves.
Chop the peppermint leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or a pair of scissors.
Steep the chopped peppermint leaves in a teapot or infuser with 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the peppermint tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the vocal cords, which can contribute to a lost or hoarse voice.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the throat and airways, allowing for smoother breathing and reduced vocal strain.
Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial compounds, such as gingerols and paradols, help combat bacterial infections that can cause a lost or hoarse voice, such as laryngitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with lost voice because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea soothes and calms the throat, reducing swelling and discomfort that can cause voice loss. Ginger's natural warming properties also stimulate blood flow to the throat, promoting healing and regeneration of tissues. As a result, ginger tea can help restore a person's voice by alleviating the underlying causes of a lost voice, making it a popular remedy for singers, public speakers, and anyone experiencing vocal strain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a lost voice.

Shogaols: Shogaols, such as 6-shogaol, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for voice recovery.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help relax the muscles in the throat and vocal cords, reducing strain and promoting a smooth voice recovery.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and restore voice.
Roots: Roots are used as they contain a high amount of active compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the throat, allowing for a smoother voice.
Buds: Buds are often used because they are rich in antioxidants and volatile oils that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, thereby restoring lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 grams of dried zingiber officinale roots in an airtight container for storage.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste before serving the warm tea immediately.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours in a sealed container.

remedy_properties: Ephedrine content: Ma huang tea contains ephedrine, a natural decongestant and bronchodilator that helps to relieve nasal congestion and open up airways, which can help to reduce swelling in the vocal cords and alleviate hoarseness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ma huang tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, which can contribute to a loss of voice or hoarseness.

This can help to soothe and calm the vocal cords, making it easier to produce sound. remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Ephedrine helps alleviate lost voice by its vasoconstrictor and bronchodilator properties, which can help improve airflow and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to speak.

Ephedradine: Ephedradine, an alkaloid compound, may aid in restoring a lost voice by its expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and other obstructions from the throat, allowing for more comfortable and effective vocalization.

Lignans: Lignans, a type of phenolic compound, may contribute to the restoration of a lost voice by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce throat inflammation and promote healing, ultimately leading to improved vocal function.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high ephedrine content, which helps to stimulate the voice and relieve vocal cord inflammation.
Leaves: They are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm and protect the vocal cords.
Stems: They are used due to their high alkaloid content, particularly ephedrine, which can help to stimulate and restore the voice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 grams of dried ephedra sinica herbs for every 8 oz of water.
Boil 8 oz of water for 3-5 minutes or until it reaches a rolling boil.
Steep the ephedra sinica herbs in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to avoid any potential bitterness.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in an airtight container for later use.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for lost voice because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm the throat.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.

Gently bruise the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to release their oils and flavor.
Steep the bruised taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.

Lignans: Lignans, a type of phenolic compound, may aid in lost voice by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes and vocal cords.

remedy_recipe: Dry the silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a single layer on a paper bag for 24 to 48 hours.
Crush the dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the silybum marianum powder with boiling water in a ratio of 1 teaspoon to 8 ounces of water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving in a cup.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tea contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from inflammation and dryness, which can contribute to hoarseness or loss of voice.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce swelling and redness in the throat and vocal cords, making it easier to speak and restore a normal voice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, teas helps with lost voice because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of the medicinal herb althaea officinalis in late summer or early fall.
Clean and dry the roots to remove any dirt or moisture for storage.
Weigh out one teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water for steeping.
Steep the root in boiling water for five to seven minutes to release its properties.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds or 3 to 4 sprigs of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers.

Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated throat tissues and reduce swelling. remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried st john's wort flowers and leaves for every cup of boiling water.
Heat filtered water to a boil in a pot and pour it over the measured herbs.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Drink one cup of the warm tea three times a day, after meals and before bedtime.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried cranberry (vaccinium macrocarpon) and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground cranberry powder with 8 ounces boiling water in a teapot or infuser for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard the solids.
Add honey to taste to sweeten the tea, mixing well to combine, and drink immediately while still warm.
Repeat the tea preparation twice daily for up to 7 days as needed, taking note of any improvements in vocal quality.

remedy_recipe: Harvest ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.

Combine 1 tablespoon of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems with 8 oz boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour it over the herbs.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with lost voice because its soothing properties calm the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and discomfort. intro_desc: Herbal teas for lost voice are natural remedies that help alleviate vocal strain, inflammation, and irritation, thereby restoring one's voice. The best herbal teas for lost voice include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Echinacea purpurea, Trifolium pratense, Lavandula angustifolia, Sambucus nigra, Rosmarinus officinalis, Verbascum thapsus, and Silybum marianum.
